steps.title: Identify the y-intercept from the given point
steps.content: The line passes through $(0, 14)$. Since the $x$-coordinate is 0, this point is on the $y$-axis, which means the $y$-intercept is $b = 14$.

steps.title: Apply slope-intercept form
steps.content:
With slope $m = \frac{1}{9}$ and $y$-intercept $b = 14$, substitute into the slope-intercept form:
$$y = \frac{1}{9}x + 14$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
